response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer (15+ years across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems) with solid fundamentals, TDD discipline, and open-source leadership. However, the role is a Principal/IC6 position specifically centered on experimentation platforms, growth engineering, telemetry, and statistical measurement — areas where the resume shows essentially no direct experience. There is also no evidence of principal-level scope: leading multi-team, multi-workstream platform initiatives or building systems adopted across many teams. Strong generalist, wrong specialization and level for this role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across multiple languages (TypeScript, DLang, C/C++, Java) meeting the core language bar
High-scale production experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering-quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tools authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-measurement platform experience — the core of this role
No demonstrated statistical/data-analysis depth (metrics design, bias detection, guardrails, experiment analysis)
Career trajectory is senior/staff IC on product teams; no evidence of principal-level, cross-team platform ownership or stakeholder alignment at enterprise scale

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering fundamentals and scale experience
